description: Overall, my experience with PFLB has been positive. Their commitment to quality and professionalism shines through in their services. The user-friendly interface allows even those who aren't performance testing experts to navigate the platform with ease. I found it particularly beneficial that PFLB supports integration with existing tools like JMeter, which made it easier for my team to leverage our previous work without starting from scratch.believe PFLB is an excellent choice—just be prepared to consider how their offerings fit your specific requirements and budget!

The team was exceptionally skilled and quickly pinpointed several issues within our system. Their insights were invaluable, leading to significant enhancements in our application's performance. They ensured that we stayed on track and consistently met our objectives. The communication throughout the entire process was clear and effective, which made collaboration seamless. In summary, PFLB's professionalism and dedication to delivering high-quality results truly stood out. If you're searching for dependable performance testing services, I highly suggest reaching out to PFLB!
cons: While PFLB offers a robust set of features, some users have noted that certain aspects of their service could benefit from more customization options. For example, while their standard testing scenarios are effective, businesses with unique requirements might find it difficult to tailor tests to fit specific needs without additional assistance or resources.

description: My experience with PFLB has been very positive. The platform provides a comprehensive set of load testing tools that help minimize risks of application failures under heavy load. PFLB is a reliable choice for improving application performance and stability.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: PFLB efficiently simulates high user traffic to identify and fix performance issues. It supports JMeter and Postman tests from the cloud, offers geo-distributed and gRPC load testing, and integrates smoothly with CI/CD pipelines. The trend reports are useful for performance tracking, and the support team is responsive and helpful.
cons: The pricing may be higher for smaller companies, but the platform’s features justify the cost. There is a learning curve, but the support team is great, and more detailed documentation would be beneficial. The platform currently lacks browser load testing.

review_title: Comprehensive Performance Testing with a Steep Learning Curve
description: My overall experience with PFLB has been positive. Their focus on performance testing and quality automation is impressive, and their use of real user data from tools like Google Analytics, Postman, Insomnia, and HAR files enhances the accuracy of their testing. The ability to simulate authentic conditions is particularly valuable for ensuring that applications perform well under various loads. However, the complexity of data integration and the steep learning curve for some tools can be challenging at times. Despite these challenges, PFLB's thorough approach to testing and quality assurance is commendable.
author: Jesus Sanchez A.
pros: What I liked most about PFLB is their focus on performance testing and quality automation, especially their ability to use real user data through tools like Google Analytics, Postman, Insomnia, and HAR files to create accurate load profiles and simulate authentic conditions during testing. This approach not only enhances the accuracy of tests but also helps ensure that applications perform correctly under various loads before being deployed to production
cons: What I liked least about PFLB is that, at times, the process of integrating data and configuring the tools can be complex and require a considerable amount of time to adjust to the specific needs of the project. Additionally, the learning curve for using some of these tools can be steep, which can make rapid implementation of tests challenging, especially for those who are not familiar with them.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Integrating PFLB with our CI/CD pipeline has completely smooth and stress free, we can now identify performance issues early enough in the development cycle. One of its incredible stand out feature is the low-code editor, we have been able to create really complex load tests without having to write any code. This way, everyone on our team regardless of their coding skill or experience can easily access this. Also, we have been able to simulate traffic from 20 different locations with the Geo-distributed load testing feature, ensuring maximum performance of our applications worldwide. We have frequent weekly catch ups where we review PFLB trend reports, to check for recent updates and how they affect the performance of our apps capacity and I have to say that the reports we get are simply awesome! Finally I would like to state how easy it has been implementing PFLB, its cloud based infrastructure has saved us resources by eliminating the need to set up and manage our load testing infrastructure, we can now focus more on improving our application.

review_title: A Powerful, Versatile Tool for Load Testing at Scale
description: Overall, PFLB has significantly boosted our ability to conduct thorough and effective load testing. The platform excels in minimizing risks associated with high user loads by providing realistic traffic simulations and identifying potential performance bottlenecks. The seamless integration with our CI/CD pipeline has been a huge advantage, enabling us to catch and address issues early in the development cycle. The additional features, like gRPC load testing, have proven to be essential for our needs, making PFLB a critical part of our performance testing toolkit. Despite a slight learning curve, the platform’s capabilities and the exceptional support make it a top choice for any team looking to ensure their applications are ready to scale.
author: Donatus U.
pros: What stands out most about PFLB is its robust and versatile set of features that cater to various load testing needs. The platform's ability to execute JMeter load tests directly from the cloud is a game-changer, making complex testing scenarios simple to implement. I particularly appreciated the geo-distributed load testing feature, which allowed us to simulate traffic from multiple global locations. This ensured that our application performs consistently well across different regions, which is critical for our international user base. The ease with which I could upload Postman collections for API testing, combined with the detailed performance metrics and reports, provided deep insights that were invaluable for optimization.
cons: One aspect that could use improvement is the onboarding experience. For those who are new to load testing or unfamiliar with PFLB, the array of features can be a bit daunting at first. Although the platform is user-friendly, it took some time to fully understand and utilize all the available tools.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: PFLB: A Game-Changer for Performance Testing
description: PFLB has been quite helpful to me. It helped in reducing the probability that an application would malfunction or crash during periods of high user traffic. It also helped in analyzing the behavior of websites and apps during loading, and in simulating high user traffic. Thanks to PFLB, I could easily import and run JMX files, perform JMeter load tests from the cloud, and get excellent results and real-time performance data through Grafana dashboards. The no-code script builder, API testing features, and testing tools provided were all helpful in improving our testing processes. This allowed us to mimic traffic from eighteen distinct places, verifying that our software works properly everywhere. Our shift was made simple by the PFLB's smooth CI/CD integration. PFLB offers superior customer support, cloud-based architecture, and superior reporting compared to competing platforms like Octoperf, Blazemeter, and Loadview. I would highly recommend PFLB to anyone in need of such software.
author: Monica N.
pros: PFLB helped minimize application failure risks and keep them under user load. The platform provides real-time performance data and detailed reports. The Grafana dashboards are nice, I could stress-test my API by uploading Postman collection to PFLB. They had a lot of nice tools that made my work easier, faster and clear. There was a no-code script builder, which helped everyone, including those that don’t know coding. There are many more, but the best part was the customer support. They were there whenever we need them.
cons: The price is too high compared to others, it does not support browser load testing, and it can be difficult for individuals who are not familiar with PFLB tools.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
